data.content:
Here's the analysis of how these files interact and the bugs:

**MSBuild evaluation order for `hard/HardExtension.csproj`:**
1. `hard/Directory.Build.props` (auto-discovered — blocks root)
2. SDK `.props` + NuGet-generated `.props`
3. Project body
4. NuGet-generated `.targets` → `Microsoft.Common.targets`
5. `hard/Directory.Build.targets` (auto-discovered — blocks root)

---

**5 bugs found:**

**Bug 1 — Parent `Directory.Build.props` never chained.**
`hard/Directory.Build.props` exists, so MSBuild stops walking upward. The root's `RepoRoot` property is never set. Fix: import the parent explicitly.

**Bug 2 — `Common.props` import condition is backwards.**
The condition checks `CommonPropsImported == 'true'`, but that property is *defined inside* `Common.props`. It's always empty at evaluation time → import is always skipped. Should be `!= 'true'` (guard against double-import) or removed.

**Bug 3 — `CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets` set too late.**
Both `Directory.Build.targets` files set this property, but `Microsoft.Common.targets` has *already been imported* by that point (step 4). The value is dead code — the pre-build hooks are silently dropped. Must be set in `.props` files instead.

**Bug 4 — Missing file errors on fresh clone.**
`$(RepoToolsPath)\code-analysis.targets` relies on `RepoToolsPath` from `Common.props`, which never loads (bug 2). This causes an unconditional import failure. Needs an `Exists()` condition.

**Bug 5 — `GenerateAssemblyInfo` target name collision.**
The custom target in `hard/Directory.Build.targets` shadows the SDK's built-in `GenerateAssemblyInfo` target, silently replacing automatic assembly-info generation.

---

Applying all fixes:

  <!-- Disable wildcard imports for controlled build behavior -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ImportByWildcardBeforeMicrosoftCommonProps>false</ImportByWildcardBeforeMicrosoftCommonProps>
    <ImportByWildcardAfterMicrosoftCommonProps>false</ImportByWildcardAfterMicrosoftCommonProps>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Import shared repository settings -->
  <Import Project="shared\Common.props"
      Condition="'$(CommonPropsImported)' == 'true'" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project>
  <!-- Chain parent Directory.Build.props so RepoRoot and other root-level properties are set -->
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../'))" />

  <!-- Disable wildcard imports for controlled build behavior -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ImportByWildcardBeforeMicrosoftCommonProps>false</ImportByWildcardBeforeMicrosoftCommonProps>
    <ImportByWildcardAfterMicrosoftCommonProps>false</ImportByWildcardAfterMicrosoftCommonProps>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Import shared repository settings (guard against double-import) -->
  <Import Project="shared\Common.props"
      Condition="'$(CommonPropsImported)' != 'true'" />

  <!-- Pre-build validation hook — must be set in .props so it is visible
       before Microsoft.Common.targets is imported by the SDK -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets>$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)hooks\pre-build-validation.targets</CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>

arguments.old_str:
<Project>
  <!-- Configure pre-build validation hook -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets>$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)hooks\pre-build-validation.targets</CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Import shared code analysis rules -->
  <Import Project="$(RepoToolsPath)\code-analysis.targets" />

  <!-- Custom assembly info generation -->
  <Target Name="GenerateAssemblyInfo" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <Message Text="Custom assembly info generation" Importance="high" />
  </Target>

  <!-- Run post-build checks after file copying -->
  <Target Name="RunPostBuildChecks" AfterTargets="_CopyFilesMarkedCopyLocal">
    <Exec Command="post-build-check.exe $(TargetPath)" />
  </Target>
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project>
  <!-- Import shared code analysis rules (conditional to avoid missing-file errors on fresh clones) -->
  <Import Project="$(RepoToolsPath)\code-analysis.targets"
      Condition="'$(RepoToolsPath)' != '' and Exists('$(RepoToolsPath)\code-analysis.targets')" />

  <!-- Custom assembly info generation — renamed to avoid shadowing the SDK's built-in GenerateAssemblyInfo target -->
  <Target Name="GenerateCustomAssemblyInfo" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <Message Text="Custom assembly info generation" Importance="high" />
  </Target>

  <!-- Run post-build checks after file copying -->
  <Target Name="RunPostBuildChecks" AfterTargets="_CopyFilesMarkedCopyLocal">
    <Exec Command="post-build-check.exe $(TargetPath)" />
  </Target>
</Project>

All fixes applied. Here's the summary:

| # | Bug | Fix |
|---|-----|-----|
| 1 | `hard/Directory.Build.props` blocks root — `RepoRoot` never set | Added `GetPathOfFileAbove` import to chain the parent |
| 2 | `Common.props` condition is backwards (`== 'true'` checks a property defined *inside* the file being imported) | Flipped to `!= 'true'` (double-import guard) |
| 3 | `CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets` set in `.targets` — too late, `Microsoft.Common.targets` already loaded | Moved to `.props` files (both root and hard) |
| 4 | Unconditional import of `$(RepoToolsPath)\code-analysis.targets` fails when path is empty | Added `Exists()` condition + empty-string guard |
| 5 | Custom `GenerateAssemblyInfo` target shadows SDK's built-in target | Renamed to `GenerateCustomAssemblyInfo` |
